title: &String → &str deref coercion: spec contradicts non-goals
description: ferrum-introduction-to-strings.md states: 'String dereferences to &str. Any function that takes &str accepts &String automatically' with an example showing word_count(&owned) where owned: String and word_count takes &str. This is a deref coercion. But ferrum-lang-core.md §1 non-goals explicitly lists 'Implicit coercions of any kind'. The bd memory also records this as a deliberate design decision: deref coercions are excluded. The strings doc is wrong and will confuse callers. Either: (a) the decision changed and the non-goals must be updated, or (b) the strings doc must be corrected (callers must write word_count(owned.as_str()) or word_count(&*owned)).
